headline: Palo Alto Networks Is Beating Palantir by 60% This Year. Is a Rebalancing Imminent?
summary: The story for investors who own shares of both Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) and Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) this year has been a study in divergence. Palo Alto Networks stock is up 2% midday Friday, extending a year-to-date gain of 41%. Palantir stock, by contrast, is essentially flat today and is down 23% year to date. ... Palo Alto Networks Is Beating Palantir by 60% This Year. Is a Rebalancing Imminent?

headline: 3 Large-Cap Stocks on Our Watchlist
summary: Large-cap stocks usually command their industries because they have the scale to drive market trends. The flip side though is that their sheer size can limit growth as expanding further becomes an increasingly challenging task.

raw.headline: 3 Large-Cap Stocks on Our Watchlist

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Palo Alto Networks (PANW) After Its Recent 44% Surge
summary: Is Palo Alto Networks at US$252.92 priced for perfection, or does it still offer value? This article walks through key signals you can use to judge that for yourself. The stock has returned 6.2% over the past week, 44.6% over the last 30 days, 41.0% year to date and 35.9% over the past year, which naturally raises questions about how much of the story is already reflected in the share price. Recent coverage has focused on Palo Alto Networks as a major cybersecurity player, with investors...

headline: Zscaler's Q3 Earnings Countdown: Buy, Hold or Sell the Stock?
summary: ZS' Q3 results are likely to reflect benefits from strong AI security demand, Zero Trust growth and momentum in Z-Flex adoption.
